item: CP3 cross-system label experiment (graze vs absorption on the nonlocal ring)

design_prestated.prestated: labels/windows frozen before the ensemble (module docstring)

bootstrap.scheme: paired percentile, resample runs within cell

validation.gate_3_seeds_per_cell: 9/9 logged tau reproduced bit-for-bit with stop_eps disabled
validation.full_ensemble_anchor: 900/900 runs reproduce logged tau bit-for-bit (t_camp == tau)

mean_field_comparators_sec3.first_crossing_recovery: up to 98% of first crossings recover (A=0.5, N=8-64)
mean_field_comparators_sec3.lifetime_ratio: tau_abs/tau_graze ~ 2.2 at the operating corner (63-67 s -> 139 s)

robustness_sweep.one_liner: across all 27 (rho_rec, W, T_v) combinations and all three cells the over-trigger fraction stays within 2-20% and the MLE lifetime ratio within 1.018-1.047; no combination approaches the mean-field gap.

verdict: NULL TRANSFER: the graze/absorption gap does not appear on the ring. First-passage and absorption-grade labels essentially coincide: over-trigger 3.0-10.3% of runs (vs 69-97% of firings / up to 98% of first crossings in the mean field), exponential-MLE lifetime ratio tau_abs/tau_fp = 1.019-1.044 (CIs exclude 1 but the shift is 2-4%, vs ~2.2x in the mean field), and 0.03-0.11 recovered excursions per run (vs 3.3-12.3 firings per run). The campaign 50-t.u. sustain label is already absorption-grade: in 0/900 runs does a campaign-labeled death later recover (t_abs is never after t_camp); where the two differ (22-36% of runs) absorption starts at most 83 t.u. EARLIER (sub-threshold descent wiggles that never re-establish contrast), moving MLE lifetimes by ~1%. The graze phenomenon is a property of the mean-field breathing dynamics, not of first-passage labeling per se.
